creator: lew@perftech.com
text:
I had a similar event, tracking down why the trusted root certificates were broken on our embedded system, which relies on the old linkage.

The problem appears to be that, beginning with ca_root_nss version 3.108, the symlink has changed, but the trusted root certificate file is still in the prior location.

In other words, we now have this linkage:

  /etc/ssl/cert.pem -> ../../usr/local/etc/ssl/cert.pem

However, there is no such target file in the package.  The package still contains the prior trusted root certificate file in /usr/local/share/certs/ca-root-nss.crt, but the symlink no longer points to this file.

This results in all OpenSSL applications that rely on the trusted root certificate store to fail.

I see a comment above that the default certificate file for OpenSSL, however, we don't find this to be the case.  Calling X509_get_default_cert_file() returns "/etc/ssl/cert.pem".
